name: Praveen Chaulagain
title: Senior Data Engineer
base_skills: Languages and Frameworks: Python, Java, Go, Scala, SQL, R, Shell scripting (Bash), Perl, Next.js, React, PySpark, Apache Spark, Apache Flume, Apache NiFi, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, Keras, XGBoost, LightGBM, Random Forest, Neural Networks
Cloud Platforms: AWS (Glue, EMR, Lambda, S3, Redshift, CloudFormation, API Gateway), Azure (Databricks, SQL Database, Data Lake, Blob Storage), GCP (BigQuery, Dataflow, Pub/Sub, Cloud Functions)

Databases and Data Warehousing: MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Redshift, Google BigQuery, Amazon Aurora, CosmosDB, Neo4j, Dimensional Modeling, ER Modeling, Star Schema, Snowflake Schema, Data Vault, Kimball methodology

Data Engineering and Pipelines: AWS Glue, Apache Airflow, Snowflake, Talend, Informatica, Prefect, Kafka, Kinesis, Spark Streaming, Amazon Kinesis, AWS Data Pipeline, Google Cloud Dataflow, Apache Beam, AWS Lambda, Azure Functions
DevOps and CI/CD: Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, Git, GitHub, GitLab, Terraform, CloudFormation, Ansible, Puppet, Chef
Data Visualization and BI Tools: Tableau, Power BI, Apache Superset, Looker, D3.js, QlikView, SAP BusinessObjects, MicroStrategy
Data Governance and Security: Data Quality (Validation, Cleansing, Profiling), Data Lineage, IAM, Encryption, Data Masking, SFTP, Files.com, APIs
Optimization and Performance Tuning: Query Optimization (SQL, Spark), Data Indexing, Cost Optimization (AWS, GCP, Azure)
Testing and Quality Assurance: Unit Testing, PyTest, JUnit, Test Automation (PyTest, JUnit), Data Validation Tools (Great Expectations)

Specialized Expertise: Salesforce (APIs, batch ingestion pipelines for Salesforce data), Healthcare Data (FHIR APIs, HL7v2, ICD-10, SNOMED CT, LOINC, RxNorm), RAG Infrastructure (Building and scaling RAG infrastructure for AI-powered applications), Gremlin (Proficiency in Gremlin for querying graph databases), Web Scraping (Social media web scraping in production environments), dbt (dbt Labs: Production experience with dbt Labs, building models and documenting Snowflake data models)
